items.title: Error Codes — Complete Reference
headings: Error Handling Strategy for SEEN V2
items.snippet: Error Codes — Complete Reference All WhatsApp Business API errors follow a consistent structure. Understanding the codes prevents wasted debugging time. Error R…
items.body: Error Codes — Complete Reference All WhatsApp Business API errors follow a consistent structure. Understanding the codes prevents wasted debugging time. Error Response Structure --Top-Level Error Codes Graph API General Errors --WhatsApp-Specific Error Codes (13xxxx) 130xxx — Throttling & Rate Limits 131xxx — Message Send Errors 131063-131064 — Template Enforcement 132xxx — Template Errors 133xxx — Account Registration Errors 134xxx — Template Sync Errors (Send-time) --Template Validation Error Subcodes These appear as when creating/editing templates: --Messenger Error Codes --Error Handling Strategy for SEEN V2

items.title: Messenger & Instagram Messaging API
items.snippet: Messenger & Instagram Messaging API The Messenger Platform powers both Facebook Messenger and Instagram Direct. Unlike WhatsApp, which is heavily template-drive…
items.body:
Messenger & Instagram Messaging API The Messenger Platform powers both Facebook Messenger and Instagram Direct. Unlike WhatsApp, which is heavily template-driven and monetized per conversation, Messenger and Instagram rely on a 24-hour standard messaging window. The 24-Hour Messaging Window When a user sends a message to your Facebook Page or Instagram Professional Account, a 24-hour timer starts. Within 24 hours: You can send standard messages, media, and interactive elements (like generic templates) for free.
After 24 hours: You can only send messages if they qualify for specific Message Tags (e.g., , , ). Promotional messages outside the 24-hour window are strictly prohibited unless sent as Sponsored Messages (Ads). Shared Features (Messenger & Instagram) Instagram-Specific Features Parameters & Fields (Sending a Generic Template) The Generic Template (Carousel) is the most common rich UI element across both platforms. Validation Rules Webhooks Messenger and Instagram webhooks share the exact same format and are delivered to the same endpoint. Crucial SEEN V2 Note: Because the payloads look identical, you must check the flag to ignore messages the bot itself sent, and look for vs events if using the Handover Protocol (passing control between Bot and Human agent). QA Checklist [ ] Ensure SEEN V2 tracks the 24-hour window timer for every active conversation per user (PSID/IGSID).
[ ] Prevent agents or automated broadcasts from sending untagged messages after 24 hours.
[ ] Implement support for Instagram Story Mention webhook payloads natively in the Flow Builder.

items.title: Message Templates
headings: Send-time Errors (when sending a template message)
❌ Rejected: Body starts with a variable (dangling)
✅ Valid: Edit a rejected template (change body)
❌ Rejected: Trying to change template name (immutable)
items.snippet: Message Templates Templates are pre-approved message formats required for business-initiated conversations outside of a 24-hour customer service window. Every m…
items.body: Message Templates Templates are pre-approved message formats required for business-initiated conversations outside of a 24-hour customer service window. Every marketing, utility, or authentication message sent first must exist as an approved template. Endpoints --CREATE — Parameters Required Optional --CREATE — Validation Rules --Components — Detail & Limits HEADER (optional) BODY (required) FOOTER (optional) BUTTONS (optional) > ⚠️ Desktop visibility rule: Templates with 4buttons, or Quick Reply buttons mixed with other types, are hidden on WhatsApp Desktop. Visible on mobile only. > UI rule: When total buttons > 3, only the first 2 show — rest hidden under "See all options." --EDIT — Rules What CAN be changed What CANNOT be changed (Immutable) Edit Rate Limits --DELETE — Rules Delete Restrictions --Error Codes Reference Creation / Validation Errors Send-time Errors (when sending a template message) --Code Examples ✅ Valid: Create a UTILITY template ❌ Rejected: Name with uppercase letters ❌ Rejected: Body starts with a variable (dangling) ❌ Rejected: Footer has a variable ✅ Valid: Edit a rejected template (change body) ❌ Rejected: Trying to change template name (immutable) --SEEN V2 — Notes > QA Note: When testing template creation, always include sample values () for any template with variables, or the API will reject with a validation error.

items.title: WhatsApp Webhooks
items.snippet: WhatsApp Webhooks Webhooks are HTTP callbacks that Meta sends to your server when events occur — a message is received, a template status changes, a user's phon…
items.body: WhatsApp Webhooks Webhooks are HTTP callbacks that Meta sends to your server when events occur — a message is received, a template status changes, a user's phone number changes, etc. You must verify your endpoint and handle each event type correctly. Setup Requirements --Verification Flow (One-Time Setup) When you save the webhook URL in your App Dashboard, Meta sends a request: ✅ Correct Response ❌ Wrong Responses --Signature Verification Every webhook POST includes the header . You must validate it to confirm the payload is from Meta: > ⚠️ Warning: Use the raw request body for HMAC calculation — NOT . Body parsers may reformat the JSON. --Webhook Fields — Subscription Subscribe to fields in your App Dashboard or via API: --Webhook Event Types — Inbound Messages Fires when a user sends your business a message. Payload structure: Message Types in Webhook --— Message Status Updates Fires when a message you sent changes status. Status Flow --Fires when a template's review status changes. --Fires when WABA-level changes occur. --Validation Rules --Error Codes in Status Webhooks --SEEN V2 Notes

items.title: WhatsApp Business Profile API
headings: Live Preview — What It Looks Like
items.snippet: WhatsApp Business Profile API The Business Profile API allows SEEN V2 to programmatically manage the public-facing profile of a WhatsApp Business Account (WABA)…
items.body:
WhatsApp Business Profile API The Business Profile API allows SEEN V2 to programmatically manage the public-facing profile of a WhatsApp Business Account (WABA). This is what end-users see when they click on the business name in WhatsApp. Managing the Profile You can read and update the profile details using the edge on the Phone Number ID. Parameters & Fields (Updating Profile) Validation Rules Live Preview — What It Looks Like ✅ Correct Usage — Updating Profile Why it works: Valid URL formats, less than 3 websites, valid vertical enum. ❌ Common Mistake — Invalid Website Format Error: — URLs must begin with or . Profile Picture Updating the profile picture requires a 2-step process using Resumable Uploads, not a direct URL. 1. Create an Upload Session: POST to 2. Upload the file: POST binary data to the returned session ID
3. Update the profile: Pass the returned handle to SEEN V2 Implementation Notes Settings UI: SEEN V2's settings dashboard should strictly validate these inputs client-side (especially URL schemes and max character counts) before hitting the Meta API to prevent frustrating errors.
Caching: Fetch the profile on app load and cache it. Do not fetch it dynamically on every page render. QA Checklist [ ] Validate URLs have [ ] Enforce max length of 139 chars for the field
[ ] Ensure vertical selection uses Meta's exact enum values, not arbitrary strings

items.title: WhatsApp Campaigns & Broadcasts
headings: Live Preview — What It Looks Like
✅ Correct Usage — Sending a Marketing Broadcast
items.snippet: WhatsApp Campaigns & Broadcasts In WhatsApp, you cannot send arbitrary free-form promotional text to users whenever you want. Any business-initiated conversatio…
items.body:
WhatsApp Campaigns & Broadcasts In WhatsApp, you cannot send arbitrary free-form promotional text to users whenever you want. Any business-initiated conversation must begin with an approved Message Template. This is the foundation of Broadcasts and Campaigns in SEEN V2. How Broadcasts Work To send a broadcast to 1,000 users, SEEN V2 iterates over the list and fires the API using a pre-approved template for each user. 1. Create a template in Meta Business Manager (or via API).
2. Wait for approval (usually seconds to minutes).
3. Execute the campaign by sending the template to the audience. Template Categories (Pricing Impacts) When creating templates, they must be assigned a category. Meta prices conversations based on this category. Marketing: Promotions, offers, newsletters. (Highest cost)
Utility: Transaction updates, shipping alerts, account notifications.
Authentication: OTPs, verification codes. (Lowest cost) Parameters & Fields (Sending a Template) The Components Array If your template has variables like in the body, or requires an image in the header, you must pass them in . Validation Rules Live Preview — What It Looks Like ✅ Correct Usage — Sending a Marketing Broadcast Why it works: The template expects an image header and two body variables (Name and Discount). The payload maps these exactly. ❌ Common Mistake — Missing Component Variables Error: — If was approved with body text "Your order {{1}} is arriving on {{2}}", sending it without the array will fail validation instantly. WhatsApp Quality Limits (The Penalty Box) When executing massive campaigns, Meta monitors user feedback. If users block or report your broadcast:
1. Tier Drop: Your messaging limit (e.g., 10K/day) can be instantly downgraded to 1K/day.
2. Template Pause: Meta will flag the specific template as . Sending it will return an error until it's unpaused.
3. Number Ban: Continuous low quality results in complete restriction. SEEN V2 Implementation Notes Batching: WhatsApp API restricts concurrency. For a 100K user campaign, SEEN V2 must implement rate limiting (max 80 requests per second) to avoid HTTP 429 Too Many Requests.
Failures: Not all numbers have WhatsApp. SEEN V2 must gracefully handle and mark the contact accordingly so they are skipped in future broadcasts. QA Checklist [ ] Ensure SEEN V2 maps exactly the correct number of variables per component
[ ] Enforce language code case sensitivity ( not )
[ ] Implement a queue system for large broadcasts to adhere to rate limits
[ ] Read webhook statuses (, , , ) to update campaign analytics in real-time

items.title: WhatsApp Flows v4 — Interactive Forms
headings: Live Preview — What It Looks Like
Comparison: Before vs After This Feature
items.snippet: WhatsApp Flows v4 — Interactive Forms WhatsApp Flows v4 introduces native interactive forms inside WhatsApp conversations, letting businesses collect structured…
items.body:
WhatsApp Flows v4 — Interactive Forms WhatsApp Flows v4 introduces native interactive forms inside WhatsApp conversations, letting businesses collect structured data without redirecting users to external links. Overview Flows v4 enables businesses to build multi-screen form experiences that run natively inside WhatsApp. Users answer questions, select options, and submit data — all without leaving the chat. For SEEN V2, this is directly relevant to lead capture, onboarding, and survey workflows in the Broadcasts module. How It Works Business sends a Flow message via the Messages API
WhatsApp renders the interactive screens on the user's device
User submits the form — a webhook payload arrives with the collected data
SEEN V2 maps the payload to a contact record or conversation Parameters & Fields Validation Rules Live Preview — What It Looks Like ✅ Correct Usage Why it works: Flow is , is , token is valid length, all required fields present. ❌ Common Mistake Error: — must be or , and exceeds 64 characters. Comparison: Before vs After This Feature Code Example — Full Working Request SEEN V2 — Impact & Notes > 📋 Linked Changelog Article: This feature was detected on 2026-06-24.
> See: Changelog entry for 2026-06-24 QA Checklist [ ] Verify Flow is in state before sending
[ ] Test the ✅ accepted example — confirm interactive message renders in WhatsApp
[ ] Test the ❌ rejected example — confirm error 100 for invalid [ ] Verify webhook receives structured payload on flow completion
[ ] Test with exactly 64 chars — confirm it works

items.title: WhatsApp Interactive Messages (Buttons & Lists)
headings: Live Preview — What It Looks Like
items.snippet: WhatsApp Interactive Messages Interactive messages replace traditional text-based "Reply 1 for Yes" workflows with native UI elements. They reduce friction, eli…
items.body:
WhatsApp Interactive Messages Interactive messages replace traditional text-based "Reply 1 for Yes" workflows with native UI elements. They reduce friction, eliminate typing errors, and improve conversion rates. For SEEN V2, interactive messages are core to the Flow Builder and Bot modules. Types of Interactive Messages 1. Reply Buttons: Up to 3 quick-reply buttons (e.g., "Yes", "No", "Talk to Sales").
2. List Messages: A menu of up to 10 rows (e.g., product categories, store locations). Parameters & Fields Reply Buttons List Messages Validation Rules Live Preview — What It Looks Like ✅ Correct Usage — Reply Buttons Why it works: Exactly 2 buttons, titles are under 20 chars, IDs are unique. ❌ Common Mistake — Too Many Buttons Error: — Maximum of 3 buttons allowed. For 4options, use a message instead. Webhooks (Receiving the Response) When a user clicks a button or selects a list row, Meta sends a webhook to SEEN V2. Important SEEN V2 implementation note: The bot builder should match against the (not the title) for routing logic, as the ID is immutable while the title might change based on localization. QA Checklist [ ] Verify button titles do not exceed 20 characters
[ ] Verify maximum 3 buttons per message
[ ] Verify list rows do not exceed 10 across all sections combined
[ ] Verify row titles do not exceed 24 characters
[ ] Ensure (for lists) is under 20 characters
[ ] Map webhook accurately to SEEN V2 Flow Builder triggers

items.title: WhatsApp Media Messages (Images, Video, Docs)
headings: Live Preview — What It Looks Like
✅ Correct Usage — Sending Image via URL
❌ Common Mistake — Wrong Media Type Key
The Media Upload API (Alternative to URLs)
items.snippet: WhatsApp Media Messages Sending media is essential for rich communication in SEEN V2. Media can be sent either by providing a public URL or by uploading the med…
items.body:
WhatsApp Media Messages Sending media is essential for rich communication in SEEN V2. Media can be sent either by providing a public URL or by uploading the media to Meta's servers first and passing the . Media Types & Limits Meta imposes strict limits on file sizes and supported formats. Sending unsupported formats or oversized files will result in immediate rejection. Parameters & Fields When sending media via URL: When sending media via ID: Validation Rules Live Preview — What It Looks Like ✅ Correct Usage — Sending Image via URL Why it works: URL is a direct link to a , and caption is under 1024 characters. ❌ Common Mistake — Wrong Media Type Key Error: — The key inside the JSON must match the . If is , the object must be , not . ❌ Common Mistake — Exceeding Size Limit Error: If the file at the link exceeds 16MB, Meta will try to download it, fail the size validation, and send an error webhook asynchronously indicating the download failed due to size limits. The Media Upload API (Alternative to URLs) If you don't want to expose media via public URLs, upload it to Meta first. 1. Upload: Returns: 2. Send: SEEN V2 Implementation Notes URL vs ID: Always prefer for static assets (logos, marketing banners) and for dynamic/sensitive assets (invoices, reports) that shouldn't be publicly accessible on your CDN.
GIF Support: WhatsApp does not natively support GIF media types for business API. You must convert GIFs to and send them as . QA Checklist [ ] Ensure user uploads in SEEN V2 are validated against Meta's strict MB limits before API dispatch
[ ] Implement auto-conversion for common incompatible types (e.g. converting to )
[ ] Verify that document filenames include the correct extension for WhatsApp to render the appropriate icon

items.title: WhatsApp Quality Rating & Messaging Limits
items.snippet: WhatsApp Quality Rating & Limits Meta enforces a strict quality control system to prevent spam on WhatsApp. If a business sends unwanted messages, users will bl…
items.body:
WhatsApp Quality Rating & Limits Meta enforces a strict quality control system to prevent spam on WhatsApp. If a business sends unwanted messages, users will block or report them. Meta tracks these blocks/reports to calculate a Quality Rating. Messaging Tiers (Limits) Every WhatsApp Business Account starts with a specific messaging limit. This is the maximum number of business-initiated conversations (Marketing, Utility, Auth) you can open in a rolling 24-hour window. 1. Tier 1: 1,000 unique customers / 24 hours.
2. Tier 2: 10,000 unique customers / 24 hours.
3. Tier 3: 100,000 unique customers / 24 hours.
4. Tier 4: Unlimited unique customers. Note: User-initiated conversations (Service conversations) do not count towards these limits. Quality Ratings (Status) Your phone number has a Quality Rating visible in the WhatsApp Manager (and via API/Webhooks). High (Green): Normal.
Medium (Yellow): Warning.
Low (Red): Danger. If your rating hits "Low", your number's status changes from to . The "Flagged" Penalty When your number is :
You cannot upgrade to a higher messaging tier.
If your rating doesn't improve within 7 days, your messaging limit is downgraded (e.g., from 10K back down to 1K).
If it remains low continuously, Meta may permanently disable the number. Upgrading Limits Meta automatically upgrades your limit (e.g., Tier 1 -> Tier 2) within 24 hours when both of the following are true:
1. Your quality rating is NOT Low.
2. The cumulative total of business-initiated conversations you've opened over the last 7 days is greater than half of your current limit (e.g., > 500 for a 1k limit). Validation Rules & API Behavior SEEN V2 Implementation Notes Throttling: SEEN V2's Broadcast engine MUST check the current messaging tier before queuing 10,000 messages on a Tier 1 account. If it blasts 10K messages, 9,000 will fail with , wasting API calls and potentially getting flagged for abuse.
Webhooks: SEEN V2 should listen for the and webhooks to alert the SEEN V2 user if they enter the "Red" zone or a template gets paused due to negative feedback. QA Checklist [ ] Ensure SEEN V2 tracks the 24-hour rolling volume of outbound broadcast messages.
[ ] Implement an alert banner in the SEEN V2 dashboard if the webhook payload drops to or .
[ ] Prevent sending broadcast messages if the template status is .
